According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 110 is again an attachment-only continuation. The required files are present, and I used only the current attachments: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md.

Turn 110 ‚Äî attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 109:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH,¬ÝNO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismŒ¶:ŒìS‚ÜíŒìH commuting with the canonical projections to G, equivalently œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The attached sweep records the stronger F=P‚ãäG-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF such thatŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Since rS and rS‚àí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this descends toŒìS=F/‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©‚âÖG

F/‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©=ŒìH.This is exactly the requested over-G isomorphism.

The proof chain remains frozen:H1 -Nielsen¬Ýequivalence+¬Ýresidual¬Ýrestricted-Lie¬Ýinduction+¬Ýnormalized¬ÝKoszul‚ÄìWitt¬Ýcoefficient¬Ýinduction+¬ÝCertificates¬ÝD.1‚ÄìD.4+¬Ýfinite¬Ýfiltered¬ÝNielsen¬Ýcompactness‚üπŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.The final compactness mechanism is still the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite solution sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; their compatible inverse limit gives Œ¶:F‚ÜíF over G, and intersecting all PN,J gives Œ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.No new structural move is warranted under the current inputs. In particular, there is still no reason to reopen the residual Magnus tower, degree 16, the A2 -layer, the t-layer, the Koszul‚ÄìWitt coefficient tower, or any Certificate D subcase. Turn 108 explicitly formalized the attachment-only transition rule: with only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger attached, and no independent measured nonlinear Certificate D artifacts, the state transition is idempotent:(ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH,¬ÝNO_MEASURED_INPUT)‚Ü¶(ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH,¬ÝNO_MEASURED_INPUT).The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts: no D.1‚ÄìD.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs independent of the history ledger. Therefore the strict Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. A PASS would require actual measured D.1‚ÄìD.4 artifacts matching the frozen contract; a FAIL would require actual measured artifacts contradicting it. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not themselves newly attached measured artifacts for the strict gate.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 110 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 109. The theorem coordinate remains ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH, via an over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF satisfying Œ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1‚ÄìD.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1‚ÄìD.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN110-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
